_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier from Hussar (L5) to Veteran Hussar (L6, Tier 2 top). Take the L5 composited cavalry lancer and produce the L6 'Veteran Hussar' variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Veteran Hussar (Tier 2 top) within-tier with 5 adds: (1) full breastplate extension downward to cover the entire abdomen, livery now reduced to chest band only between bevor and breastplate top edge, (2) short steel tassets at hips, livery hem becomes skirt band, (3) layered pauldrons (second smaller disc on top of L4 disc on each shoulder), (4) steel sabatons on boots, (5) iron peytral plate replacing L4 studded leather peytral on horse chest. Locked composite thresholds 15/35 pool=12. Spatial-geometry directional language only (SPEAR-SIDE/REIN-SIDE). NEW: explicit no-white-markings rule on horse (locked 2026-05-06 after L5 keeper-pick) — horse is solid chestnut brown across entire body, no blaze, no star, no socks, no speckling.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0. (a) Tassets revised from narrow articulated lames to 2 WIDER rectangular plates per hip (per Detail Economy rule — fewer wider lames carry the upgrade better than fragmented narrow lames). (b) Boot armor at L6 is ANKLE PLATES ONLY (not full sabatons) — full sabatons moved to L8 per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier' (locked split: L4 toe-cap → L6 ankle plates → L8 full sabatons). (c) L5 spear-haft reinforcement plate references removed from preserve block (was REMOVED at L5). (d) Preserves the full-arm scale-mail (L5+) — scale-mail covers bicep-to-wrist with vambraces sitting on top. (e) References to L3 knife sheath stripped (removed at L3 per AD).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy (drift-corrected pass).
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v7 (2026-05-07): tighten L6 against detail-density bleed: (a) reframe L6-3 LAYERED PAULDRONS -> THICKER TALLER PAULDRONS (single-piece volume bump, no stacked-disc construction); (b) strip explicit decorative rivet enumeration from all 5 L6 positive adds (breastplate rim, tasset seam+upper, pauldron join, ankle-plate seam, peytral upper+lower); (c) add EDIT GOAL preamble + tightened NEGATIVE block clauses against layered construction + decorative rivet rows + surface ornament.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.

v9 (2026-05-07): remove chest band of livery (between bevor and breastplate's upper edge) per user feedback — cloth-above-armor looks impractical and reads weird. Breastplate now sits flush against the bevor with no cloth visible above. Livery presence preserved via the SKIRT BAND (between breastplate bottom and tassets top) + the horse caparison.
v9 (2026-05-07): residual chest-band PRESERVE/preamble references scrubbed.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

adds.title: FULL BREASTPLATE EXTENSION DOWNWARD
adds.summary: extend the L5 partial steel breastplate downward to cover the entire abdomen, ending at the natural waist where it meets the leather belt.

adds.title: SHORT STEEL TASSETS AT THE HIPS
adds.summary: 2 WIDER LAMES PER TASSET — add a tasset hanging from the leather waist belt at each hip (one tasset on the spear-side hip, one tasset on the rein-side hip).

adds.title: THICKER TALLER PAULDRONS (VOLUME BUMP — SINGLE-PIECE)
adds.summary: grow each existing pauldron into a thicker, taller domed cap (~25% taller, ~15% wider), single-piece — NOT a stacked-disc construction, NOT two discs riveted together. Volume + silhouette change carries the upgrade per AD detail-economy.

adds.title: STEEL ANKLE PLATES ON BOTH BOOTS
adds.summary: add a neutral silver-gray steel matching the L1 kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) ankle band wrapping each riding boot at the ankle, sitting ABOVE the existing L4 iron toe-cap strip on the front of the toe.

adds.title: IRON PEYTRAL PLATE REPLACING THE L4 STUDDED LEATHER PEYTRAL
adds.summary: replace the existing L4 studded leather peytral on the horse's chest with a single articulated neutral dark-gray iron matching the L1 kettle-helm tone (balanced gray) plate covering the same area (from the base of the horse's neck down to the front of the saddle).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L5/composite/cavalry_L5_v4_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — [DETAIL-ECONOMY GUARDRAIL: every positive add below is a single-piece coverage extension or a single-piece volume bump. NO layered plate construction. NO 'second disc on top' / 'stacked discs' / 'smaller upper plate' framing. NO enumerated decorative rivet rows on plate edges, plate seams, peytral edges, or boot seams. Plate seams read as clean steel-on-steel or steel-on-leather joints; visible rivets only where structurally required by the existing baseline (kettle-helm brim seam) — nowhere new at this level.] Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the FIRST input image, marking the within-tier promotion from Hussar to Veteran Hussar. The rider already wears the existing Hussar kit: kettle-helm, mustache (no beard, clean-shaven cheeks/chin), small steel bevor at the throat, partial steel breastplate covering the upper third of the torso (with the yellow+red surcoat livery still visible BELOW the breastplate as a skirt band down to the waist belt), FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves covering both arms bicep-to-wrist, two matching steel pauldron discs on both shoulders, steel vambraces on both forearms wrist-to-mid-forearm sitting ON TOP of the scale-mail, leather waist belt with plain leather belt, leather grip cord and iron banding rings + reinforced socket on the wooden spear haft, iron pommel cap at the haft butt, hip pouch on the spear-side hip (the only belt accessory — no knife sheath), iron toe-cap strip on the front of each riding boot, blue trousers, brown leather knee-high riding boots in stirrups. The horse already wears: small steel chamfron on forehead, iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, studded leather peytral on the chest beneath the red+blue checkered caparison. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings, no blaze, no star, no socks, no speckling. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the rein hand grips the reins, the side closest to the horse's head and mane. Throughout this prompt, do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement; use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) FULL BREASTPLATE EXTENSION DOWNWARD — extend the existing partial steel breastplate downward to cover the entire abdomen, ending at the natural waist where it meets the leather belt. The breastplate is now a single piece of neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) covering the rider's torso from the collarbone (top edge of the existing breastplate, unchanged) down to the waist belt. The medial ridge from continues down the new lower section to the belt. The lower edge of the breastplate is rounded where it meets the belt (clean steel-on-leather joint, no decorative rivets). The breastplate's UPPER EDGE sits flush against the bevor's LOWER EDGE — NO cloth visible between them, NO chest band of livery above the breastplate (the cloth-above-armor pattern looks impractical and was removed 2026-05-07). The yellow+red surcoat is fully covered by the breastplate from the bevor's lower edge down to the breastplate's lower edge. The remainder of the surcoat (between the breastplate's bottom edge and the saddle line below the belt) is now the SKIRT HEM of the surcoat, also reduced to a band — see Edit Goal item (2). (2) SHORT STEEL TASSETS AT THE HIPS — 2 WIDER LAMES PER TASSET — add a tasset hanging from the leather waist belt at each hip (one tasset on the spear-side hip, one tasset on the rein-side hip). Each tasset is built from TWO WIDER RECTANGULAR STEEL LAMES stacked vertically (NOT 3+ narrow lames — wider plates, fewer of them,). Upper lame ~3 inches tall and ~6 inches wide, lower lame ~3 inches tall and ~7 inches wide with a slight outward flare at the bottom edge. The two lames are joined at a clean steel-on-steel seam where the lower lame overlaps the upper lame (rounded corners). The upper edge of the top lame attaches to the belt at a clean steel-on-leather joint (no decorative rivet rows). The tassets cover the upper thigh below the belt. The hip pouch (on the spear-side hip) remains visible attached to the belt ABOVE the tasset — the tasset does NOT cover the pouch. The surcoat hem is now visible as a narrow SKIRT BAND between the breastplate's bottom edge (above) and the tassets' top edge (below) — approximately 3 inches tall, showing the yellow+red split panels still readable. (3) THICKER, TALLER PAULDRONS (VOLUME BUMP — SINGLE-PIECE, NOT LAYERED) — grow each existing pauldron into a thicker, taller domed cap. The existing single-disc pauldron (~5 inches diameter as visible in the input image) increases in vertical extent by approximately 25%, in width by approximately 15%, and gains a more pronounced domed curve that reads as a single thicker mass — NOT a stacked-disc construction, NOT two discs riveted together, NOT a layered second cap. Both pauldrons remain smooth single-piece domed neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) matching the existing pauldron color, simply more voluminous. The shoulder line reads visibly broader and taller at thumbnail scale (silhouette mightiness rule). NO new rivets, NO seam line dividing the pauldron into two pieces, NO sculpted ornament on the dome surface. (4) STEEL ANKLE PLATES ON BOTH BOOTS — add a neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) ankle band wrapping each riding boot at the ankle, sitting ABOVE the existing iron toe-cap strip on the front of the toe. Each ankle plate is approximately 2 inches tall, articulated where the boot bends at the ankle, joining the leather at a clean steel-on-leather seam (no decorative rivets at the seam edge). The brown leather body of each boot remains visible at the calf, at the heel, and across the instep BETWEEN the existing toe-cap (front of toe) and the new ankle band (above the ankle). FULL SABATONS (continuous toe + foot top + ankle steel coverage) DO NOT arrive — at the boot armor is split: toe-cap (front of toe only) PLUS new ankle band (around the ankle only); the instep is still leather between them. Per 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier.' (5) IRON PEYTRAL PLATE REPLACING THE STUDDED LEATHER PEYTRAL — replace the existing studded leather peytral on the horse's chest with a single articulated neutral dark-gray iron matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray) plate covering the same area (from the base of the horse's neck down to the front of the saddle). The new iron peytral is solid steel rather than studded leather, with a smooth slightly-curved painted-steel surface and clean steel-on-leather seams at the upper edge (where it wraps the base of the neck) and the lower edge (where it meets the saddle girth) — no decorative rivet rows. The studded-leather grid pattern from is fully gone — replaced by smooth iron with seam-edge rivets only. The new iron peytral sits BENEATH the caparison's chest panel (caparison still drapes the horse's body unchanged). ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; confident flat closed-mouth expression; thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris; prominent slightly-hooked nose; warm peachy-tan skin tone with soft rosy blush; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE handlebar-shaped covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow; dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle-helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the steel kettle-helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, open face. Smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail. THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged in shape and position. THE RIDER TORSO: the steel breastplate (partial breastplate now extended at to cover the entire abdomen down to the waist belt — see Edit Goal item 1); the yellow+red split surcoat livery now visible ONLY as a SKIRT BAND between the breastplate's bottom edge and the new tassets' top edge (~3 inches tall, both panels distinguishable). The wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle and the EXISTING plain leather (no studs), unchanged. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves covering both arms bicep-to-wrist; the EXISTING steel vambraces on both forearms wrist-to-mid-forearm sitting ON TOP of the scale-mail at the forearm (unchanged); both bare hands warm peachy-tan skin (the spear-side hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord; the rein-side hand grips the brown leather reins at the horse's withers). Both shoulders have the EXISTING matching steel pauldron discs (unchanged) — the new layered second disc on TOP of each is added per Edit Goal item (3). THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff (visible at the calf below the new tassets and below the saddle skirt on the spear-side); chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf — the existing iron toe-cap strip on the front of each toe remains, and the new ankle plate adds a steel band at each ankle per Edit Goal item (4); the brown leather body of each boot remains visible at the calf, the heel, and across the instep between the toe-cap and the new ankle band (full sabatons not). THE WEAPON: the long wooden spear with iron leaf-tip — heavy warm-brown wooden haft, neutral gray (matching the existing kettle-helm tone, balanced gray) iron leaf-shaped spearhead at the top, held vertically in the rider's spear-side hand at shoulder height with the spear-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. The iron pommel cap, leather grip cord wrap, and iron banding rings + reinforced socket on the haft all remain unchanged. (No reinforcement plate — that beat was removed.) THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit back to the rider's rein-side hand at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed. THE HORSE: the SOLID CHESTNUT-BROWN chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the rein-side of the frame and rump and tail on the spear-side of the frame, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the rein-side of the head with a bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — the muzzle, forehead, neck, chest, belly, legs, hooves, and rump are ALL the same warm chestnut brown color. NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze on the muzzle, no white star on the forehead, no white socks on the legs, no white speckling, no white spots, no white shading. The dark brown mane along the top of the neck and the dark brown tail are slightly darker than the chestnut body but neither contains any white. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, tail, and ears all remain identical in shape and position to the input image. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with the EXISTING iron banding on pommel and cantle (unchanged); the brown leather bridle with the EXISTING iron browband and iron square buckles (unchanged); the EXISTING small steel chamfron forehead plate on the horse (unchanged) sitting OVER the bridle's headstall; the new iron peytral plate per Edit Goal item (5) replaces the existing studded leather peytral and sits BENEATH the caparison's chest panel. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares) and steel-blue trim band along the lower edge with shallow rectangular tabs — UNCHANGED, identical to the input image. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: all eight belt studs; iron pommel cap on spear butt; iron browband and bridle buckles; hip pouch on spear-side hip; leather grip cord on spear haft; iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle; single steel pauldron disc on each shoulder; iron banding rings + reinforced socket on spear haft; studded leather peytral on horse chest; iron toe-cap strip on each boot; FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist; steel breastplate upper-chest portion; steel vambraces on both forearms layered ON TOP of scale mail; small steel chamfron on horse forehead; steel bevor/collar at rider's throat — ALL preserved unchanged in shape and position (other than the existing breastplate's downward extension and the existing peytral's replacement per Edit Goal items 1 and 5). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees toward the viewer's left so the rider's body presents to camera. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord wrap; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the iron leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas. Rider+horse vertically centered. The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the iron leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading, no environmental context, no scenery, minimal contact shadow under the horse's hooves only. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white forehead patch, no white muzzle patch, no white socks, no white speckling, no white spots, no white shading anywhere on the horse; the horse is SOLID chestnut brown across the entire body including the muzzle, forehead, legs, and hooves; DO NOT add a white background gradient, white aura, or white glow around the horse; DO NOT add a chest band of cloth, livery, surcoat, or any fabric between the bevor's lower edge and the breastplate's upper edge — the breastplate sits flush against the bevor with no cloth visible above the breastplate; DO NOT cover the new skirt band of livery between the breastplate's bottom edge and the tassets' top edge — both yellow and red panels must remain visible in the skirt band; DO NOT extend the tassets above the waist belt — they hang BELOW the belt; DO NOT extend the tassets to mid-thigh or longer (they grow longer at this level); DO NOT break the tassets into 3 or more narrow lames — each tasset is built from EXACTLY 2 wider rectangular lames; DO NOT cover the existing hip pouch with the tasset — the existing pouch remains visible above the spear-side tasset at the rider's hip; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory at this level; DO NOT add a closed helm, sallet, half-visor, full visor, helm crest, helm horns, or any structural addition to the kettle-helm — the helm stays exactly as the existing kettle-helm at this level (sallet arrives at this level); DO NOT remove the kettle-helm or replace it with a different helm; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT split any plate into two stacked pieces or layered construction — the existing pauldron volume bump is a SINGLE-PIECE thicker dome (NOT two stacked discs); the existing breastplate extension is a SINGLE-PIECE downward extension of the existing breastplate (NOT a separate lower plate); the existing tassets are 2 wider lames per tasset (the lame count is locked at 2 wider plates; do NOT add a third smaller upper plate or a stacked decorative cap); DO NOT enumerate decorative rivet rows along plate edges, seam lines, lame overlaps, peytral perimeters, ankle-plate seams, or breastplate-belt joins — every plate seam reads as a clean steel-on-steel or steel-on-leather joint without visible rivet enumeration; DO NOT add sculpted ornament, embossed motifs, raised filigree, engraved patterns, or any surface decoration on the new pauldron domes, tassets, breastplate, ankle plates, or peytral — the existing plate surfaces are smooth painted steel; DO NOT render any new metal piece in cool blue-gray steel, bluish steel, icy silver, chrome, OR overly warm/brown-tinted steel — every new metal addition must match the NEUTRAL gray tone of the existing kettle-helm and spear leaf-tip (balanced gray, NEITHER blue-shadowed NOR brown-shadowed; soft painted highlights that read clean-white, neither cool nor warm); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow — cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven with NO beard at all; DO NOT change the surcoat colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boot color remains warm-brown at the calf, heel, and instep — full sabatons are NOT added at this level, only ankle plates are; full-foot continuous steel coverage arrives at this level); DO NOT add a continuous full sabaton (toe + foot top + ankle as one continuous steel surface) at this level — the boot armor at is the ankle band ONLY, with the existing toe-cap remaining as a separate piece on the front of the toe and the leather instep visible between them; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the spear; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the reins; DO NOT change the spear shape or replace the wooden haft with a steel lance haft (steel haft arrives at this level); DO NOT add a pennon flag or banner to the spear (that arrives at this level); DO NOT remove the existing leather grip cord, the existing iron banding rings, or the existing reinforced socket on the spear haft (no reinforcement plate — that beat was removed); DO NOT shrink, replace, or remove the FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves at the upper arm — the scale-mail covers the entire arm bicep-to-wrist with vambraces ON TOP at the forearm; DO NOT add gauntlets at this level (gauntlets arrive at this level); DO NOT extend the steel vambraces past mid-forearm or remove them; DO NOT remove or hide the the existing pauldron discs underneath the new layered upper discs — both layers (lower + new upper) must be visible on each shoulder; DO NOT make the new layered upper disc the same size as the lower disc — the upper disc is smaller (~3 inches vs the existing disc's ~5 inches); DO NOT add gold trim, gold studs, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT remove the existing small chamfron on the horse's forehead; DO NOT extend the chamfron over the horse's muzzle or eyes (it stays as the small forehead plate); DO NOT add a crinet or flanchards (those arrive); DO NOT replace the iron peytral with leather again — at the peytral is solid iron plate; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT change the horse's mane shape, tail shape, ear shape, or neck arch; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, the steel-blue trim band, or the tabbed lower edge; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure or the horse; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane other than the existing minimal contact shadow; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
